days.title: Arrival — ease in near the hotel
days.summary: She's flying in tired. Everything tonight is within 15 minutes' walk of Waterloo St.

days.title: Gardens by the Bay — the big one
days.summary: Slow start. Afternoon at the domes, sunset from the Supertrees, dinner at Satay by the Bay, finish with Garden Rhapsody.

days.title: Chinatown, CBD, Marina Bay waterfront
days.summary: All clustered southwest of the hotel. Temples + Maxwell at midday, AC nap, Telok Ayer + Lau Pa Sat satay, Spectra at 9pm.

days.title: Little India + Kampong Glam
days.summary: Both walkable from the hotel. Easy recovery day with a long siesta between.

days.title: Civic District + Peranakan dinner
days.summary: Elegant Singapore — National Gallery, Esplanade roof, Violet Oon for the splurge dinner.

days.title: Botanic Gardens, Orchard, river at sunset
days.summary: West in the morning, AC malls in the afternoon, bumboat cruise from Clarke Quay at golden hour, crab at Jumbo.

days.title: Katong + Jewel sendoff
days.summary: Slow Katong morning, the famous laksa, late checkout, Jewel until your near-midnight flight.

places.name: Hotel Waterloo Singapore
places.description: Your base for the week — 78 Waterloo St, recently rebranded Handwritten Collection. Central, walkable to Bugis, Kampong Glam, Bras Basah, Civic District.
tips: Request a 1pm late checkout for Fri 22 May.
Bag storage available after checkout.

places.name: Albert Centre Market & Food Centre
places.description: 5-minute walk from the hotel. Easy first taste of hawker culture. Try bak kut teh near the back, soon kueh, chwee kueh, and tau suan for dessert.
places.pregnancyNotes: Bak kut teh is peppery pork rib soup, fully cooked and comforting. All recommended dishes here are fully cooked.
tips: Closed Thursdays — fine for Saturday arrival night.
Use a tissue packet to reserve a seat — local custom.

places.name: Kampong Glam — Sultan Mosque & Haji Lane
places.description: Gentle post-dinner stroll. Sultan Mosque lit up beautifully at night, then murals, lanterns, and shophouses along Arab Street and Haji Lane.
tips: Stop at Maison Ikkoku or any Haji Lane café for a mocktail / fresh juice.
Back to hotel by 9:30pm — first night.

places.name: Toast Box, Bugis Junction
places.description: Classic kaya toast set, 5 min walk from the hotel. The toast and kaya are perfect; the eggs are the only thing to swap.
places.pregnancyNotes: Skip the half-boiled eggs that come with the set — ask for fully cooked or hard-boiled. Cap kopi/teh at ~2 cups/day.

places.name: National Museum of Singapore
places.description: 12-min walk from the hotel. The History Gallery is air-conditioned, manageable, and sets context for the week.
tips: Great rainy-day backup if today's plan shifts.

places.name: Flower Dome (Gardens by the Bay)
places.description: Cool, AC, magical. Hit this first — Tulipmania closes today. MRT Downtown Line: Bencoolen → Bayfront, 10 min.

reservation.notes: Combo ticket: Flower Dome + Cloud Forest + Supertree Observatory. Book on Klook, Pelago, or official site. Aim for 2:30–3pm entry.

places.name: Cloud Forest
places.description: Mist, AC, the Jurassic World exhibition, indoor waterfall. Second stop after Flower Dome.
tips: Take an AC rest break on the benches between domes.

places.name: Supertree Grove & Observatory
places.description: Observatory at sunset (~6:45pm). Stay for Garden Rhapsody at 7:45 or 8:45pm — free 10-min synchronised light & music show. Sit on the grass at OCBC Garden Plaza.
tips: Garden Rhapsody runs nightly, no booking.

places.name: Satay by the Bay
places.description: Hawker centre inside the Gardens. Chicken satay, Hainanese chicken rice, sugar cane juice — fully cooked, casual, perfect timing before Rhapsody.
places.pregnancyNotes: Satay is well-grilled, safe. Skip any oyster omelette or rojak stalls.

places.name: Ya Kun Kaya Toast, Bugis Junction
places.description: Alternative to Toast Box — heritage chain, same kaya-toast set. 8 min walk from the hotel.
places.pregnancyNotes: Hard-boiled eggs, not the half-boiled default.

places.name: Sri Mariamman Temple
places.description: Singapore's oldest Hindu temple — the colourful gopuram on South Bridge Road. 244 South Bridge Rd.
tips: Shoes off at the entrance.

places.name: Buddha Tooth Relic Temple
places.description: Striking red-and-gold temple with a free rooftop garden. Slow walk through Pagoda St and Smith St to get here.
tips: Rooftop garden has a cool, quiet vibe — quick AC-free breather.

places.name: Maxwell Food Centre
places.description: 7-min walk from the temples. Pregnancy-friendly hit list: Ah Tai Hainanese Chicken Rice (#01-07, ex-Tian Tian, shorter queue), Marina South Delicious fried carrot cake (cooked egg), Lao Ban soya beancurd for dessert.
places.pregnancyNotes: Ah Tai's chicken is poached then chilled — fully cooked. Skip any oyster omelette and rojak stalls.

places.name: Thian Hock Keng Temple
places.description: Old Hokkien temple on Telok Ayer St. Stroll continues along Amoy Street and Ann Siang Hill — restored shophouses, café-lined lanes.
tips: Quick Grab here from the hotel after the nap.

places.name: Lau Pa Sat & Satay Street
places.description: Victorian cast-iron market. Walk the indoor stalls first, then head to Satay Street (Boon Tat St) at 7pm when the grills fire up. Stalls 7 & 8 are the famous ones.
places.pregnancyNotes: Chicken & beef satay, nasi lemak, sugar cane juice — all well-cooked, safe.

places.name: Marina Bay waterfront — Spectra show
places.description: Free light & water show off MBS Event Plaza, nightly at 8pm and 9pm. Watch from the Helix Bridge or the boardwalk in front of the Merlion for the best reflection.
tips: 15 min walk from Lau Pa Sat, or 5 min Grab.

places.name: Merlion Park
places.description: Classic skyline shot across to MBS. Visit en route to / after Spectra.
tips: Crowded — go to the lower platform for clearer water-level photos.

places.name: Chye Seng Huat Hardware
places.description: Industrial-style café in Jalan Besar, 15 min walk. Proper Western-style brunch and good coffee for a change of pace.
tips: Closed Mondays — Tuesday is fine.

places.name: Tekka Centre
places.description: Little India's wet market downstairs, food court upstairs. South Indian thosai, biryani, roti prata with chicken curry, sweet lassi.
places.pregnancyNotes: Skip the rojak (raw veg/fruit). Sweet lassi over kopi if you've already had your caffeine cap.

places.name: Sri Veeramakaliamman Temple
places.description: One of Singapore's oldest Hindu temples, dedicated to Kali. 141 Serangoon Rd — 5 min walk from Tekka.

places.name: House of Tan Teng Niah
places.description: The rainbow shophouse — your Little India photo stop. 37 Kerbau Rd.
tips: Pair with Sri Veeramakaliamman + Tekka in one loop.

places.name: Zam Zam
places.description: Open since 1908. Legendary murtabak — chicken or mutton stuffed flatbread, griddled to order. 697-699 North Bridge Rd.
places.pregnancyNotes: Murtabak filling is fully cooked. Safe.

places.name: Hjh Maimunah
places.description: Alternative to Zam Zam: a Malay nasi padang spread, point-and-pick the lauk. Beef rendang, ayam masak merah, sayur lodeh.
places.pregnancyNotes: All slow-cooked dishes — safe. Skip the raw sambal belacan side.

places.name: Mr Stork (Andaz Singapore)
places.description: Rooftop with tepee-style seating, 360° view over Kampong Glam and Bugis. Calm vibe, good mocktail menu.
tips: 10-min walk from the hotel. Reservations recommended on weekends, walk-in fine on Tue.

places.name: The Populus Café
places.description: Brunch-style start near Outram. Antipodean coffee, all-day brunch plates.
tips: A short Grab from the hotel — about 10 min.

places.name: National Gallery Singapore
places.description: Best Southeast Asian art collection in the world, set in the old Supreme Court + City Hall. Heavily AC. 2–3 hours easy. Casual lunch options on B1.
tips: 5 min walk from the hotel.
Free for Singapore residents; ticketed for tourists.

places.name: Esplanade Roof Garden
places.description: Free, take the lift up Esplanade Mall. Sweeping view of Marina Bay, much less crowded than MBS, perfect golden hour before Violet Oon.
tips: Plan to be up here by 6:30pm.

places.name: National Kitchen by Violet Oon
places.description: Modern Peranakan in a stunning heritage room inside the National Gallery. Try beef rendang, kueh pie tee, dry laksa.
places.pregnancyNotes: Everything well-cooked. Skip raw chilli sambal on the side if she prefers.

reservation.notes: Book via Chope or the official site. Aim for 7pm. Walk home (10 min) after.

places.name: Smoke & Mirrors
places.description: Rooftop bar on top of the National Gallery. View straight across the Padang to MBS — one of the city's best. Solid non-alcoholic menu.
tips: Walk-in usually fine on a weeknight.

places.name: Killiney Kopitiam
places.description: Heritage kopitiam (1919) just off Orchard. Kaya toast set, curry chicken with bread, mee siam.

places.name: Singapore Botanic Gardens
places.description: UNESCO-listed. Walk the Healing Garden and Swan Lake — flat, shaded, quiet. MRT to Botanic Gardens station.
tips: Bring water — shaded but humid.

places.name: National Orchid Garden
places.description: Paid section inside Botanic — absolutely worth it. The VIP Orchid section is air-conditioned for a heat break.
tips: ~SGD $15 entry. Pay at the gate.

places.name: PS.Cafe Dempsey
places.description: Garden setting in Dempsey Hill, lighter Western menu, gorgeous space. 10-min Grab from Botanic.
tips: Open Farm Community next door is the alternative if PS is full.

places.name: ION Orchard
places.description: AC mall central. Browse, rest, refresh between Dempsey and Clarke Quay. Takashimaya and Paragon are next door.
tips: Food hall in the basement is decent if you want a snack.

places.name: Singapore River Cruise (Clarke Quay)
places.description: 40-min bumboat ride from Clarke Quay jetty at sunset. Passes Cavenagh Bridge, Merlion, Fullerton, MBS. Seated and scenic — perfect for a pregnant traveller.

reservation.notes: Book on Klook 1–2 days ahead for the 6:30pm slot.

places.name: Jumbo Seafood, Riverside Point
places.description: Where the cruise drops you. Chilli crab and black pepper crab — a must-try, fully cooked.
places.pregnancyNotes: Skip the cockles / clam sides. Crab is fine.

places.name: Chin Mee Chin Confectionery
places.description: Legendary kaya toast in Katong — charcoal-toasted bread, kaya by the spoon. 15-min Grab from the hotel.
places.pregnancyNotes: Hard-boiled eggs. This is the breakfast for foodies.

places.name: Koon Seng Road shophouses
places.description: The pastel Peranakan shophouse row — your photo stop. Slow walk down Joo Chiat Rd for tile murals; browse Rumah Bebe.
tips: Best light before 11am.

places.name: 328 Katong Laksa
places.description: THE laksa. Noodles cut short so you eat with a spoon. Add an otah on the side. 51 East Coast Rd.
places.pregnancyNotes: Order "no cockles, please" — that's the only risky element. Prawns and fishcake are fully cooked and fine.

places.name: Jewel Changi Airport
places.description: Arrive by 4–5pm with bags stored. Stand under the Rain Vortex (free, hourly). Canopy Park on L5 (booked). Shiseido Forest Valley for the indoor garden walk.
tips: Rain Vortex Light & Sound Show at 8pm and 9pm — perfect last-Singapore moment.
Dinner at the basement food court: Five Star Hainanese, Tim Ho Wan, Burger & Lobster.

reservation.notes: Canopy Park 1-hour slot, late afternoon. Skip the Sky Nets (bouncing one is a no-go pregnant, walking one tiring).

places.name: Changi Airport (departure)
places.description: Near-midnight flight home. Changi immigration is famously fast — 5 min even at peak.
tips: Store luggage at T1 or T3 left-luggage counters (~SGD $14/bag/24h) from 3pm onwards.
Connected to Jewel by walkway / Skytrain — no Grab needed once at the airport.
